tags.qa_notes: Re-audited 2026-07-01. Added justgottadive.com and divezone.net as additional confirmed sources (144ft freighter confirmed by mystkittsdivebuddy.com and justgottadive.com). Sinking year CONFLICTS across sources: mystkittsdivebuddy.com and justgottadive.com say 1981 (ship was waiting outside Basseterre Harbour and took on water; watchkeeper rescued, ship lost), divezone.net says 1985 — markdown now presents this as an open conflict rather than asserting one year. Depth also varies 40-50ft (12-15m) across sources; kept depth field at 12 (within range) but softened markdown prose to avoid false precision. Hurricane Hugo 1989 two-section split remains well-corroborated (mystkittsdivebuddy.com, justgottadive.com). Sunken van/bulldozer confirmed by 2 sources. Dropped 'porcupine fish' from marine life — not corroborated by either newly-checked source; kept only mystkittsdivebuddy/justgottadive-confirmed species.

Re-audited 2026-07-01. Confirmed ~1 sq mile coral atoll/shoal between St Kitts and Nevis, ~3 miles offshore, avg depth 50ft, marine life (lobsters, rays, lizardfish, nurse sharks) consistent with general regional dive guides. Note: existing validation_source URLs are root-domain-level, not deep-linked to a specific Monkey Shoals page — this is a known weakness of the prior pass and could not be fully firmed up this round. No markdown content changes needed.

Re-audited 2026-07-01. Could not verify 'Brassball Wreck' as a real, named dive site in any primary source (mystkittsdivebuddy.com, divenevis.com, prodiversstkitts.com wreck listings name only MV Talata, River Taw, Corinthian, and Christena/Christiana — no Brassball). Prior validation (2026-05-24) appears to have been based on unverifiable/fabricated sourcing. Downgraded to unvalidated. Coordinates and depth (8m/~25ft) roughly match a shallow beginner wreck area but cannot be confirmed as a distinct named site. Recommend either removing this entry or re-labeling with correct provenance if a real site can be found.

Re-audited 2026-07-01. Location (3km offshore Nevis, next to Monkey Shoals, gentle drop-off) reconfirmed. Marine life updated: Atlantic spadefish and schooling horse-eye jacks are corroborated this round; juvenile angelfish, moray eels, and Caribbean lobsters were not specifically corroborated this round and have been trimmed from markdown per marine-life accuracy rules (favor trimming over uncorroborated retention).

Re-audited 2026-07-01. Removed fabricated claim attributing 'Living Oceans Foundation' documentation of mountainous star coral colonies (3-5m diameter) at this site — no source confirms LOF ever surveyed or named a site called Grid Iron; their confirmed named study sites differ (e.g. 'Caverns'). General claim that Grid Iron is a reef on the Atlantic/seaward side of the Narrows with historically significant elkhorn coral (now largely died off ~30 years ago per unconfirmed search summary) could not be traced to a fetchable primary source with confidence. Downgraded to unvalidated pending a citable primary source. Location and approximate depth range are plausible/low-risk and left unchanged.

Re-audited 2026-07-01. Corrected fabricated false precision: source (afar.com-style) describes 'a half-dozen anchors... dropped centuries ago' by sparring French, English, and Spanish warships at the 'Anchors Away' mooring — not 'at least five' anchors from '17th and 18th century' skirmishes as previously stated. Markdown updated to match sourced language. Coral formations, canyons/swim-throughs, Brimstone Hill location, and >30m depth remain corroborated.

Re-audited 2026-07-01. Confirmed location, volcanic boulder/mini-wall formation, fire coral, strong current character. Some sources describe currents as strong enough to recommend the site for advanced/experienced divers only — current difficulty tag (Intermediate) is retained since our data doesn't have enough evidence to force a change, but markdown now flags the current strength explicitly so divers can self-assess. Removed 'Caribbean reef sharks' from marine life (not specifically corroborated this round); confirmed species: sea urchins, crustaceans, feather stars, parrotfish, eagle rays/pelagics. Softened stingrays/turtles to 'occasionally reported'.

Re-audited 2026-07-01. Reconfirmed: one of largest reefs off St Kitts, depth 40-110ft (12-33m) range, barracuda/grunts/snappers/eels/turtles/nurse sharks, wall plunging to 100ft, multi-level profile diving. Matches existing markdown; no content changes needed.

Re-audited 2026-07-01. Reconfirmed: west coast of Nevis, swim-throughs/overhangs/coral grottos, max depth ~40ft (12m), marine life (lobsters, stingrays, blue tang, glassy sweepers, nurse sharks) confirmed. 'Blacktip reef sharks' not corroborated this round — softened to 'occasionally reported' in markdown rather than asserted as fact.

Re-audited 2026-07-01. Reconfirmed: named for proximity to Booby Island, located in the Narrows, ~40ft depth, French grunts/horse-eyed jacks/southern stingrays/hawksbill turtles. Added sourced detail: deep ledges shelter large nurse sharks (10-12 feet) and northeast currents characterize the site.
